title: Spermacoce bangweolensis
taxonomy: Spermacoce bangweolensis is classified as a species within the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the madder family. This classification places it within the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. Spermacoce bangweolensis is also known as the Bangweolo bush violet, and its classification helps to understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the same family. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 600 genera and 13,000 species. Spermacoce bangweolensis is just one of the many species within this family, and its classification helps to identify its unique characteristics and adaptations.

It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In some regions, the plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The plant is also believed to have antiseptic properties, which may help in wound healing.
